demographics.living_situation: lives at home with a parent, who is present and helping during the call

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My chest is so tight, I can't get the air all the way out, and my inhaler isn't working anymore.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Breathing has been getting worse for hours and the blue puffer has stopped helping even after lots of puffs.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 6 hours ago, after a cold started over the last day or two
symptom_narrative.duration: 6 hours and getting worse the whole time
symptom_narrative.character: chest feels really tight, like I can't push the air back out, with a whistling/wheezing sound when I breathe
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: worst it's ever been — I can only get out a few words before I have to stop for breath
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing right now — the blue reliever puffer used to fix it but it isn't working even after using it lots of times
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: trying to move or talk, and leaning back
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: heart is racing, breathing really fast, and I feel very anxious and frightened
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I get asthma flare-ups but I've never been in intensive care for it, and it's never been this bad
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: can't walk across the room without getting severely short of breath; sitting upright leaning forward

past_medical_history.how_managed: a blue reliever puffer and a daily preventer puffer

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: the room is dusty with pet dander and feels stuffy and poorly ventilated

objective_data_offered.value: 93% on a home fingertip pulse oximeter
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on an unvalidated home oximeter during distress; can be unreliable with movement or cold fingers
wrist wearable reading taken while distressed and moving

objective_data_offered.value: visibly breathing fast and hard, using neck and chest muscles, sitting leaning forward on his hands, only able to speak in short phrases
a whistling/wheezing sound can be heard when he breathes out
the blue reliever puffer is empty after being used many times tonight
